constraints.name: 2D Constant Cross-Section Only
constraints.description: Cold drawing and cold rolling can only produce profiles with a constant cross-section along the entire length. Any geometry that varies in the third dimension (tapers, steps, undercuts, 3D-varying) is physically impossible without downstream machining. This is an inherent limit of the drawing/rolling process.

constraints.name: Max Cold-Draw Cross-Section
constraints.description: Maximum achievable cross-sectional area via cold drawing is limited to approximately 2,500 mm² by draw force, die stress, and material flow constraints. Profiles exceeding this require hot rolling or forging.

constraints.name: Max Profile Width
constraints.description: Maximum profile width achievable is 160 mm, constrained by die geometry, draw bench capacity, and material flow stability during cold drawing.

constraints.name: Max IH Cross-Section
constraints.description: Maximum cross-section for induction hardening is approximately 4,000 mm², limited by inductor coil geometry and through-heating risk on larger sections. Cross-sections above this limit require alternative hardening methods (flame, furnace).

constraints.name: Min Carbon Content for Hardening
constraints.description: Induction hardening requires a minimum carbon content of 0.35% C to achieve the martensitic transformation necessary for surface hardness. Low-carbon structural steels and austenitic stainless grades cannot be induction hardened.

constraints.name: Steel-Only Material Scope
constraints.description: ZOLLERN's cold drawing and rolling equipment is designed exclusively for carbon and alloy steel. Titanium, aluminum, copper alloys, and non-ferrous metals cannot be processed. This is a hard equipment constraint.

constraints.name: Min Economic Batch for Custom Die
constraints.description: Custom die tooling costs €5–20K per geometry. Below approximately 100 kg total order volume, the die amortization makes per-unit cost uncompetitive versus CNC machining from bar stock. Die NRE can be charged separately to mitigate.

constraints.name: Custom Tooling Lead Time
constraints.description: New custom die design, manufacture, and first-article inspection requires 6–12 weeks. This lead time is incompatible with JIT production programs or rapid procurement cycles unless dies are pre-commissioned.

constraints.name: Induction Hardening Length Range
constraints.description: Induction hardening line accepts workpiece lengths of 2,200–9,000 mm. Parts shorter than 2,200 mm or longer than 9,000 mm cannot be processed in-house and require outsourced heat treatment or alternative hardening methods.

constraints.name: Carbon Steel Corrosion Susceptibility
constraints.description: Carbon and low-alloy steel corrodes rapidly in aqueous acidic (pH < 5) or chloride-rich (> 100 ppm Cl⁻) environments without surface treatment. Applications in wet, corrosive, or sterilization environments require protective coatings (galvanizing, chrome, nickel) or specification of corrosion-resistant grades (420SS, weathering steel).

constraints.name: High-Strength Grade Hydrogen Embrittlement
constraints.description: Quenched-and-tempered grades exceeding 1,000 MPa UTS are susceptible to hydrogen-induced stress cracking in hydrogen environments or after electroplating without proper bake-out. Requires hydrogen bake-out per ISO 9587 or grade limitation below 1,000 MPa for electroplated applications.

constraints.name: High-Temperature Oxidation Limit
constraints.description: Carbon and low-alloy steel oxidizes significantly above 450°C and structural creep begins above ~400°C. Applications adjacent to exhaust systems, furnaces, or high-temperature processes require heat-resistant ferritic/martensitic grades or thermal-barrier coatings.

constraints.name: Defence / Firearms Export Control
constraints.description: Profiles destined for firearms and defence end-use are subject to ITAR (US), EAR (US), and EU dual-use export regulations (2021/821) regardless of the steel specification. Registration and compliance programs required for US-destined defence supply.

constraints.name: Pressure Equipment Material Documentation
constraints.description: Profiles used as pressure-retaining structural members must be delivered with EN 10204 3.1 certified material test reports to comply with PED 2014/68/EU and ASME BPVC equivalents. Standard delivery does not include 3.1 certs — must be specified at order.

sources.label: 02_functional_promise.md — complements, BOM position, functional promise scope
